mnemonics: Strings are IMMUTABLE: Imagine Making Modifications? Unexpectedly, That's Actually Denied! (Because original strings don't change, you get a new one)
keyPoints: Strings in Java are immutable objects; their values cannot be changed after creation
Modification methods (like concat, replace, toUpperCase) return a brand new String object
Use StringBuilder or StringBuffer for efficient, mutable sequence of characters
commonMistakes: Using the + operator in loops for concatenation, which creates many temporary String objects and is inefficient
Assuming methods like replace() modify the original string instead of returning a new one
Not using StringBuilder for complex string manipulation, leading to poor performance
